reports.title: UK Electricity Mix Weekly Report: Saturday 13 June
reports.intro: A measured weekly report for EnergyMix.info using the available 7-day historical generation feed for Sunday 7 June to Friday 12 June.
reports.summary: The week from Sunday 7 June to Friday 12 June averaged about 23.4 GW of measured generation, with renewables averaging 51.8% of measured output. The strongest renewable day in the available data was Friday 12 June, when wind, solar and hydro together averaged 64.9% of measured generation. Gas was highest on Wednesday 10 June and lowest on Friday 12 June.
reports.takeaway: For flexible electricity use, the cleanest opportunities are likely to appear when wind is strong, gas is low and demand is not at a peak. This is the foundation for future EV-charging guidance, clean-electricity alerts and weekly newsletter summaries.
drivers: Renewables were strongest on Friday 12 June, when wind, solar and hydro averaged 64.9% of measured generation.
Wind was the largest swing factor in the available feed, peaking at about 12.0 GW average output on Friday 12 June.
Solar was strongest on Tuesday 9 June, averaging about 4.5 GW across settlement periods after embedded-solar backfill where needed.
Gas averaged about 6.0 GW across the period, rising highest on Wednesday 10 June and falling lowest on Friday 12 June.
Measured generation was highest on Wednesday 10 June, at roughly 24.4 GW average output.

cleanestPeriods.title: Best low-carbon proxy: Friday 12 June
cleanestPeriods.body: Historical carbon-intensity aggregates are not yet part of this weekly report feed, so EnergyMix.info uses renewable share and gas output as cautious proxies. Friday 12 June had the strongest renewable share at 64.9%.

cleanestPeriods.title: Lowest gas proxy: Friday 12 June
cleanestPeriods.body: Gas generation averaged about 3.9 GW on Friday 12 June. Lower gas periods are often cleaner, but the report does not yet claim this was the lowest-carbon period without historical carbon-intensity validation.

higherCarbonPeriods.title: Highest gas proxy: Wednesday 10 June
higherCarbonPeriods.body: Gas averaged about 8.8 GW on Wednesday 10 June. Gas-heavy periods are usually higher-carbon, but this is labelled as a proxy until historical carbon-intensity highs/lows are added to the report feed.

highlights.label: Wind
highlights.value: Highest average wind output was about 12.0 GW on Friday 12 June.

highlights.label: Solar
highlights.value: Highest average solar output was about 4.5 GW on Tuesday 9 June.

highlights.label: Gas
highlights.value: Highest average gas output was about 8.8 GW on Wednesday 10 June; lowest was about 3.9 GW on Friday 12 June.

reports.methodologyNote: This report is generated from the available 7-day historical generation feed and validated against external public data. Carbon-intensity highs/lows and interconnector summaries should be treated as future additions unless they are present as validated historical aggregates.

yesterday.title: UK Electricity Mix Yesterday: Friday 12 June
yesterday.summary: Yesterday, Friday 12 June, measured average generation was about 24.4 GW. Renewables averaged 64.9% of measured generation, gas averaged 3.9 GW, wind averaged 12.0 GW and solar averaged 3.5 GW.

highestRenewableShare.text: Current measured 7-day high: 64.9% average renewable share on Friday 12 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestWindGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 12.0 GW average wind output on Friday 12 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestSolarGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 4.5 GW average solar output on Tuesday 9 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestGasGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 8.8 GW average gas output on Wednesday 10 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

socialPosts.title: Monday weekly grid brief
socialPosts.body:
Last week in Britain’s electricity mix:

• Highest renewable share in the available feed: 64.9% on Friday 12 June
• Highest average wind output: ~12.0 GW on Friday 12 June
• Highest average solar output: ~4.5 GW on Tuesday 9 June
• Highest average gas output: ~8.8 GW on Wednesday 10 June

The shift was clear: gas-heavy weekdays gave way to a cleaner, wind-led weekend.

Full report: https://energymix.info/reports/weekly/2026-06-13/

socialPosts.title: Wednesday explainer
socialPosts.body:
What does “UK electricity mix” actually mean?

For live grid dashboards, it usually means Great Britain’s electricity system: England, Scotland and Wales. Northern Ireland is part of the UK, but operates in a separate electricity market.

Explainer: https://energymix.info/uk-electricity-mix

socialPosts.title: Friday practical clean-electricity post
socialPosts.body:
The cleanest time to use electricity is not a fixed hour.

It changes with wind, solar, demand, imports and gas generation. Windy overnight periods and sunny middays can be much cleaner than still early-evening peaks.

Guide: https://energymix.info/cleanest-time-to-use-electricity
